schema.from: string - sender display name and address
schema.to: string - recipient display name and address
schema.date: string - ISO 8601 date displayed in message
schema.subject: string - email subject line
schema.body: string - email body text (supports basic HTML)
schema.deliver_at: string - ISO 8601 timestamp for time-gated visibility
schema.in_reply_to: string|null - parent message ID for threading

items.subject: RE: Holiday party Friday
items.body:
Lisa,

I've got the playlist sorted. Mix of holiday stuff and some Backstreet Boys per Karen's request (don't blame me).

See you Friday!

Dave

> -----Original Message-----
> From: Lisa Milavic
> Sent: Wednesday, December 22, 1999 2:30 PM
> Subject: Holiday party Friday
>
> Dave,
>
> Are you still handling the music for Friday?
> I've got the cake ordered from Veniero's.
>
> Lisa

items.subject: Headaches
items.body:
Hey Lisa,

Is it just me or has everyone on the 14th floor been getting headaches this week? I've gone through a whole bottle of Tylenol. Dave says it's the fluorescent lights but I don't know.

Maybe it's just the stress of the holidays + Y2K.

Karen

items.subject: Y2K Readiness -- Initial Assessment
items.body:
All,

As part of our ongoing technology planning, the IT department has begun assessing CalCom's systems for Year 2000 compliance. This is a company-wide initiative and we appreciate your cooperation.

Please take a few minutes to complete the attached Y2K assessment questionnaire by January 15, 2000. The questionnaire asks about any software or equipment you use that displays or calculates dates.

If you notice any date-related irregularities in your applications, please report them to IT Support (ext. 4357) so we can include them in our review.

We expect to have a full remediation plan in place by Q2 2000. In the meantime, this is strictly a planning exercise -- no immediate action is required on your part beyond completing the questionnaire.

Thank you,

Margaret Sullivan
Director of Operations
CalCom Research Services

items.subject: Morning shift swap?
items.body:
Lisa,

Any chance you could cover my AM shift today? My daughter's school has a holiday assembly thing and I promised I'd be there.

I can take your PM slot instead.

Thanks,
Jim

items.subject: Password Expiration Notice
items.body:
Your network password will expire in 7 days.

Please change your password before December 30, 1999 to avoid being locked out of your account.

To change your password, press Ctrl+Alt+Delete and select 'Change Password'.

-- This is an automated message. Do not reply. --

items.subject: Holiday party Friday
items.body:
Dave,

Are you still handling the music for Friday? I've got the cake ordered from Veniero's.

Lisa

items.subject: Q4 Survey Status
items.body:
Margaret,

Attached is the Q4 status report. We're at 847 of 1,160 interviews completed. Should be on track to finish by the 30th.

Let me know if you need anything else.

Lisa

items.subject: Respondent concerns
items.body:
Margaret,

I wanted to flag something about the Q4 survey responses. Several respondents in the Roosevelt Island area have mentioned

items.subject: RE: Strange readings
items.body:
Lisa,

I wouldn't worry about it. The survey data looks normal to me. Sometimes respondents just have bad days.

Forget I mentioned it.

Tom

> -----Original Message-----

> Sent: Friday, December 17, 1999 3:15 PM

>

>

> respondents are you looking at?
>

items.subject: URGENT: Network maintenance tonight
items.body:
Attention all staff,

We will be performing emergency network maintenance tonight beginning at 8:00 PM. All workstations on floors 12-15 will be affected.

Please save your work and log off before 7:45 PM.

We apologize for the short notice. This maintenance is part of our initial Y2K systems assessment.

CalCom IT Support

items.subject: FW: thought of you -- wireless industry paper (preprint)
items.body:
Lisa!!

Ok I know I'm the worst at keeping in touch but I literally thought of you the second I saw this and I HAD to send it before the holidays. One of our analysts got ahold of a preprint -- it's an academic paper about the economics of the US wireless industry and how all the new PCS providers are shaking up the market. It hasn't been published yet (supposed to come out in Telematics and Informatics early next year) but the findings are already making the rounds. Very dry in places (it IS an academic paper lol) but some of it is wild.

The short version: the FCC auctioned off all this new wireless spectrum and it was supposed to create competition, but what's actually happening is all the big players are just buying each other. AT&T bought McCaw, Bell Atlantic is merging with GTE, SBC is gobbling up Ameritech -- it's like watching Pac-Man. The paper says horizontal concentration is the most prominent structural change in the whole industry. So much for competition right??

Also there's this whole section about how the new PCS entrants spent $20 BILLION on spectrum licenses alone and some of them are already going bankrupt because they can't afford to actually build the networks. And then there's the standards war -- CDMA vs TDMA vs GSM -- and if you pick the wrong one you're basically stranded.

Anyway I thought of you because you guys are right in the middle of all this with CalCom. Isn't the Horizon launch coming up? I keep seeing the ads. This paper basically describes the exact market you're launching into. Billions of dollars flying around, everyone merging, subscriber counts exploding (69 million wireless customers now!!), and average bills dropping to like $39/month because of flat-rate pricing wars.

I attached the preprint draft -- our analyst scribbled some margin notes on it but it's totally readable. Don't tell anyone I sent it lol, it's not officially out yet.

How are you?? How's Roosevelt Island? Are you still in that apartment with the radiator that sounds like a dying cat? We need to get lunch when I'm in DC next -- probably mid-January after the holidays settle down. I'll email you.

Miss you!! Have a good Christmas :)
Gina

--
Gina Ventimiglia
Research Associate
Greenfield Online, Inc.
Westport, CT
(203) 555-0283

attachment_paths.wireless_industry_analysis_draft.pdf: assets/research/An economic analysis of the US wireless.pdf

items.subject: Y2K Compliance: All Systems Nominal
items.body:
Good morning, everyone.

We are pleased to report that all CalCom systems have passed the Year 2000 rollover without incident. Servers, workstations, CATI terminals, telephony switches, and internal databases all transitioned cleanly at midnight on January 1, 2000.

This outcome reflects months of preparation by the IT department and the cooperation of every division. We would like to thank all staff who completed Y2K assessment questionnaires, reported date anomalies, and observed the December 30 log-off window. Your patience made a real difference.

A few housekeeping items as we move into Q1:

1. DATE DISPLAY: Some applications may show two-digit years as "00" rather than "2000." This is a display issue only and does not affect data integrity. We are rolling out patches on a department-by-department basis.

2. NETWORK DRIVES: If you experience difficulty accessing shared folders on \\CALCOM-FS02, please submit a ticket to ext. 4357. A small number of file permissions were reset during the rollover and we are correcting them manually.

3. HORIZON DEVICES: If you experience clock drift on your Horizon device, please contact Hardware Support at extension 4477. A firmware update is being distributed to affected units.

Once again, thank you for your cooperation. We are proud to confirm that CalCom entered the new millennium without a single critical system failure.

CalCom IT Support
Extension 4357

items.subject: Sunday shift reminders + off-island authorizations
items.body:
Lisa,

A few items for today.

1. Please complete any remaining Q4 callbacks before end of shift. The final batch report is due to the client on Wednesday and I would like all disposition codes entered by tomorrow morning.

2. Log your breaks in the shift tracker as usual. Facilities has asked us to note any time spent away from the floor so they can reconcile badge swipes with the new access system.

3. For the HCS-00-Q1 study, you have been authorized to dial a set of off-island respondents. These are Horizon device holders outside the standard Roosevelt cohort. See the authorization memo in your Surveys\Authorizations folder (OffIsland_Auth_HCS-00-Q1.doc) for the full number list and protocol notes.

4. January schedules are posted on the shared drive under \\CALCOM-FS02\Scheduling. If you need to swap a shift, please coordinate directly with the other interviewer and copy me on the confirmation.

Thank you,

Margaret Sullivan
Director of Operations
CalCom Research Services

items.subject: Lunch???
items.body:
Hey Lisa,

Please tell me you're taking a lunch break today. I cannot sit at this desk for another four hours straight. Sunday OT is cruel and unusual punishment.

Want to grab something from the deli on Main St? I'm thinking noon, maybe 12:15.

Also have you been watching the weather? They're saying there might be a big storm later this month. Like, blizzard big. I'm already dreading the commute if that happens. The tram shuts down every time it snows more than three inches.

Let me know.

Karen

items.subject: Re: Lunch???
items.body:
Karen,

Yes. Please. I need to get away from this screen for a few minutes. Meet you downstairs at 12:15.

Lisa

items.subject: question about today's last call
items.body:
Hi Margaret,

I had a question about a call I made this afternoon and I didn't want to wait until Monday to ask.

The last call of my shift was the Bergmeier number from the off-island list (202-555-3011, 4:46 PM). He picked up. Before answering my screener he asked me how confidential the line was and I told him "this is an unrecorded line." I said that because I thought I remembered it from the script flipbook and because he sounded nervous. He answered everything after that and we got a clean complete.

But after I hung up I realized I am not actually sure if that is true. Are these calls unrecorded? I always assumed everything was recorded for QA. If the line was actually being recorded and I told a respondent it wasn't, I think I have a problem, especially because of who he is or might be. He used "Rep. Bergmeier" once but answered to "Senator" without correcting me, so I am not sure which.

I would feel better knowing what I should have said and what I should say if it comes up again. I am sorry for asking on a weekend.

I am also a little confused about why the Bergmeier number was on my list. The off-island numbers you authorized for the Horizon study, are those a separate distribution from the Roosevelt Community Access Initiative? Different paperwork? Different consent language? I want to make sure I am reading the right script for the right respondents.

Thank you,
Lisa

items.subject: re: question about today's last call
items.body:
Lisa,

Thank you for flagging this. You handled the call correctly.

To clarify: all standard CATI calls are recorded for quality assurance purposes. This is noted in the interviewer handbook (Section 3.2) and in the consent language read to each respondent at the start of the screener. The recording is automatic and cannot be disabled from the workstation.

Regarding the off-island numbers, the authorization memo in your Surveys\Authorizations folder covers the distribution and protocol. Those respondents received their devices through a separate channel but are surveyed under the same study instrument. If you have further questions about the list composition, please bring them to our Monday check-in and I will walk you through it.

You have nothing to worry about. Please finish logging your dispositions for today and enjoy the rest of your evening.

Margaret Sullivan
Director of Operations
CalCom Research Services

Do not reply to this message. For questions, contact Hardware Support at extension 4477.

Ref: MX-2000-0109-0487

items.subject: Re: Lunch???
items.body:
Karen,

Thanks for lunch, I really needed that. You were right about the soup.

Hey, you mentioned something about the 14th floor headaches coming back. I was looking at the Q4 respondent notes from December and a few of them reported similar symptoms, especially the ones on the island. I'm not saying it's related but it seems like a weird

items.subject: call from a survey respondent this morning
items.body:
Hi,

I wanted to let someone in legal know about a call I received this morning on the survey line.

At approximately 7:00 AM today (January 15) I received an incoming call from a respondent named Richard Madison. He is in our system as H-0001, part of the Q4 Horizon satisfaction study. I surveyed him on January 9th. His callback number is 202-555-2984.

Mr. Madison said he had received a call from us about five days ago and that he was curious about CalCom. He mentioned that he used to be a journalist before he started teaching, and that he gets "hunches." He asked if I would be willing to speak with him on the record about CalCom.

I told him that I am not authorized to speak on behalf of CalCom or any of its subsidiaries. I offered to connect him with the legal department. He declined but said he understood and that he would save my number.

I am not sure if this is something that needs follow-up, but I wanted legal to be aware in case he does contact you directly. If he calls me again, please let me know if there is anything specific I should or should not say.

Thank you,
Lisa
